MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-17-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jeffrey G. Knowles, Esq., Coblentz Patch Dufy & Bass, LLP, Telephone 415-391-4800, appearing for Plaintiff;
Charlie Weiss, Esq., Coblentz Patch Dufy & Bass, LLP, Telephone 415-391-4800, appearing for Plaintiff;
Matthew Borden, Esq., BraunHagey & borden, telephone 415-559-0210, appearing for Defendants

Ruling - ARGUED. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendants George and Trevor McBratney, Phil Klaassen, Elaine Price, and Heather Parsons' anti-SLAPP motion to strike both causes of action alleged in the complaint filed by plaintiff John Phelps is denied as to both causes of action. Defendants fail to satisfy the first prong of the anti-SLAPP analysis. Mr. Phelps alleges that defendants illegally recorded his telephone conversations. This conduct does not involve protected activity within the meaning of CCP 425.16(e)(4). Defendants' recording of business conversations regarding a Canadian dog food company also does not concern the public interest. Courts have identified a matter of public interest as statements either concerning a person or entity in the public eye, conduct that could directly affect a large number of people beyond the direct participants or a topic of widespread public interest. (See, e.g., Rivero v. American Federation of State, County, and Municipal Employees, AFL-CIO (2003) 105 Cal.App.4th 913, 924.) The fact that the parties now argue over the admissibility of the recordings also does not implicate the anti-SLAPP statute. The complained of conduct occurred prior to any petitioning activity and the basis of liability is the act of recording. Mr. Phelps' request for fees is denied as the motion was not frivolous. Order is signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/HEK)
